steps.title: Introduce the phenomenon
steps.content: The main clause says astronomers detected a striking phenomenon. The noun phrase after the blank identifies that phenomenon as the explosion of a massive star, so punctuation should introduce an explanation.

steps.title: Avoid fragments and splices
steps.content: Choice A uses a semicolon before a fragment, Choice B creates a comma splice with it was, and Choice C leaves the noun phrase as a sentence fragment. The remaining choice uses a colon to introduce the explanation. The answer is D.
